mnemonics: TRY-CATCH the built-in EXCEPTIONS: Throwable's Kids Run Everywhere - Errors are serious, RuntimeExceptions are unchecked, and all other Exceptions are checked.
keyPoints: Java exceptions are objects that inherit from the Throwable class.
There are two main types: Error (serious, unrecoverable) and Exception (programmatic issues).
Exceptions are further divided into Checked (must be declared or handled) and Unchecked (RuntimeExceptions, not required to be handled).
commonMistakes: Catching Exception or Throwable instead of a more specific exception type, which can mask bugs.
Using exception handling for normal program flow instead of for exceptional circumstances.
Empty catch blocks that swallow exceptions, providing no feedback or recovery.
